content.full_text:
RAVI MENTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
August 28,
1996
Terry Grub
Post Office Box 108
Volcano, California
95689
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-96-254
Dear Mr. Grub:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
conflict—of—interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
“Act”) 1
QUESTION
As the member of a local district committee that reports to
the district board,
may you participate in decisions concerning
reestablishing water flow from a source that your family owned
before selling it to the district?
CONCLUSION
Yes.
You may participate in decisions regarding the
property,
because you do not currently have an ownership or
investment interest in the property.
FACTS
You have been proposed as a member of a new committee that
would report to the Volcano Community Services District board of
directors in the Town of Volcano in Amador County.
The committee
will report to the board on matters pertaining to reestablishing
water flow from a district—owned source above town that your
family used to own.
The source,
an old mining tunnel,
was owned
by your family for nearly 60 years and then leased to the district
for another 20 years.
In the early 1980s,
your family sold the
property to the district.
During the sale, the principals on your
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Commission regulations
appear at Title 2, Sections 18000-18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—96—254
Page
2
side of the family passed away and you became conservator of their
estate and received payments for their share of the sale.
The sum
owed has been paid in full for more than six years.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official from
making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.
Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:
(b)
Any real property in which the public official
has a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand
dollars
($1,000)
or more.
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in
the regular course of business on terms available to the
public without regard to official status,
aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value
provided to, received by or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when .the
decision is made.
(Section 87103(b)—Cc).)
You are concerned because you plan to serve on a new
committee that would report to the Volcano Community Services
District on reestablishing water flow from a district—owned source
of water above town.
2
Your family formerly owned the water
source,
but sold the land in the early 1980s.
The purchasers
finished making payments to you from the sale of the land
approximately six years ago.
You have no ownership interest or
investment in the property at this time and the purchasers of the
property are no longer a source of income to you.
Accordingly,
you do not have an economic interest in the land as defined by
Section 87103.
You may participate in decisions regarding the
property.
2
We do not have enough facts about the committee to determine
whether the committee will be making governmental decisions or
whether you would be considered a public official under the Act.
However,
we need not address this issue at this time to answer
your immediate question.
If you would like to explore this issue
further, please contact our office.


r .L .i
r i_i.
£
— .
—‘
Page
3
If you have any further questions, please feel free to
contact me at
(916)
322-5660.
SGC:LR: ak
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General
iph
Counsel,
Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As the member of a local district committee that reports to
the district board,
may you participate in decisions concerning
reestablishing water flow from a source that your family owned
before selling it to the district?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
You may participate in decisions regarding the
property,
because you do not currently have an ownership or
investment interest in the property.
